response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile individual contributor with nearly two decades of hands-on development and genuine developer-tooling credentials (test runners, assertion libraries, CI/CD infrastructure, MCP code generators). However, this is a Level II people-management role requiring 2+ years of people management and 3+ years of formal technical leadership, and the resume shows neither — every role listed is an IC position. Mobile exposure is incidental (one iOS/Apple TV stint) rather than platform-ecosystem depth, and there is no evidence of roadmap ownership, performance management, or leading organizational change. Great engineer, wrong role.
strengths: 19+ years of broad software development experience spanning web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems
Authentic developer-tooling track record: test frameworks (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), CI/CD and GitLab infrastructure, device orchestration tools, MCP code generators
Demonstrated knowledge-sharing and mentoring instincts through workshops, multi-module courses, and conference talks
concerns: No people-management experience at all, against a hard requirement of 2+ years for this manager role
No formal technical leadership role (3 years required); leadership evidence is limited to a side-project founder title and teaching
Thin mobile-platform depth for a mobile-ecosystem org, plus an unexplained employment gap (Jan 2020 – Sep 2020)

key_factors.factor: Hands-on developer tools and testing infrastructure expertise
No formal technical leadership or org-change experience
